top_categories.workout_context: At mile 60 of 102, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
El Tour de Tucson's flat to gently rolling Sonoran Desert rewards steady threshold power. Surging and recovering costs more watts than just holding.
Race-pace efforts that mimic El Tour de Tucson's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before November.
When someone attacks on flat to gently rolling Sonoran Desert, you have 10 seconds to respond or you're off the back. This builds that response.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 102 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of El Tour de Tucson. Train it until it feels boring.
102 miles rewards fat oxidation. This volume work shifts your fuel mix so you're still burning fat at hour 5, not bonking at hour 3.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 3,007ft of climbing on flat to gently rolling Sonoran Desert does to your power file on race day.
Two threshold sessions per week without the recovery cost of VO2max work. For 102 miles, sustainable FTP gains matter more than peak power.
El Tour de Tucson's flat to gently rolling Sonoran Desert forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 102 miles.
Tempo is El Tour de Tucson's race pace. The wattage you'll hold for 102 miles on flat to gently rolling Sonoran Desert. Train it until it's automatic.
At El Tour de Tucson, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for El Tour de Tucson’s 102 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, TT Threshold, and Race Simulation to prepare you for 102 miles of flat to gently rolling sonoran desert in Tucson, Arizona, USA.
